rulings.Case Title: VALERIE ANN CARLING et al VS. ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Federated Department Stores, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Request For Terminating Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Federated Department Stores¿ motion for terminating and monetary sanctions is granted as to terminating sanctions and denied as to monetary sanctions. The parties must appear at the hearing unless defendant¿s counsel notifies plaintiffs of the tentative ruling and plaintiffs state that they do not wish to contest the tentative ruling. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE RIGG et al VS. ASTENJOHNSON, INC., 'F/K/A ASTEN INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Deposition Testimony By Percipient Witness Deborah Ann Brady And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,955
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's MOTION To Compel Deposition Testimony By Percipient Witness Deborah Ann Brady And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,955 is off calendar. Matter to be heard in Deparment 206 at 9:30 AM. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT NELSON et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant John Crane¿s demurrer and motion to strike will be heard with line 7 on the law and motion calendar at 9:30am. Defendant¿s demurer is sustained with five days leave to amend to provide plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able, to identify each product attributable to defendant that allegedly caused injury. (Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal. 4th 71, 80). Defendant¿s motion to strike is moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. However, as guidance for the parties, absent the ruling on the demurrer, the motion to strike would be denied because there are sufficient allegations of malice, fraud and oppression and no requirement that plaintiffs¿ allegations be stated separately as to the moving defendant from the other defendants. =(220/HEK)
